title: SQL: Aborting process
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE scene (id INT PRIMARY KEY, victim_id INT NOT NULL, description VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE victim (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), age INT CHECK (age >= 0));
CREATE TABLE inspector (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), experience_years INT DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO scene (id, victim_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'Crime Scene A');
SELECT i.name, v1.name, v2.name FROM scene s JOIN scene b ON s.id <> b.id JOIN victim v1 ON v1.id = s.victim_id JOIN victim v2 ON v2.id = b.victim_id JOIN inspector i ON 1=1;
key_bug_features: Invalid SQL join condition causing process abortion
